bugs.error_message: E2E getJwt() sends {user_id} body and reads data.access_token — both wrong for Java iam-service API
bugs.root_cause: Python user-mgmt POST /auth/token expected {user_id: string} and returned {access_token}. Java iam-service expects {username, password} and returns {accessToken}.
bugs.fix: Changed body to {username: userId, password: 'Meridian@2024'} and read data.accessToken

bugs.error_message: 03-jwt-identity.spec.ts: health, JWKS, token, membership tests all fail after iam-service migration
bugs.root_cause: Tests were written for the Python user-mgmt service. Java iam-service uses GET /actuator/health, /oauth2/jwks (no 'use'/'alg'/'kid' fields), {username,password} token request, and PATCH /users/{id}/book for membership changes.
bugs.fix: Rewrote all 4 test categories for the Java iam-service API shape

bugs.error_message: 08-domain-authz.spec.ts: rm_jane and rm_diaz JWT fetch uses {user_id} and body.access_token
bugs.root_cause: Direct /auth/token calls at lines 231-265 still used old Python API contract
bugs.fix: Changed to {username, password} body and body.accessToken field

bugs.error_message: AuthController JWT missing segments, clearance, book, admin_domains, audience claims
bugs.root_cause: JWT issued by iam-service lacked claims needed by Cerbos policy evaluation and gateway identity resolution
bugs.fix: Added ObjectMapper injection, extractClearance/extractSegments helpers, book/adminDomains from UserResponse, audience(['meridian-gateway']) to JWT builder

bugs.error_message: PATCH /users/{id}/book returns 500: column 'metadata' is of type jsonb but expression is of type character varying
bugs.root_cause: Hibernate maps String field to character varying by default. The column is jsonb. Inserting null causes a type mismatch.

bugs.error_message: Gateway returns 401 for valid iam-service JWT: invalid iss and missing aud claim
bugs.root_cause: Gateway VALID_ISSUERS was hardcoded to ['meridian-user-mgmt', 'http://user-mgmt:8084']. iam-service JWT has iss=http://iam-service:8084 and no aud claim.
bugs.fix: Made required-issuers configurable via @Value (includes all iam-service URL variants). Added audience(['meridian-gateway']) to AuthController JWT builder.

bugs.error_message: JwksClient fails with 'Invalid JSON object': Java HttpClient does not follow redirects by default
bugs.root_cause: Java HttpClient.Redirect.NEVER is the default. /.well-known/jwks.json returns 301 to /oauth2/jwks. HttpClient got the redirect response (HTML/empty body), JWKSet.parse() failed.
bugs.fix: Added .followRedirects(HttpClient.Redirect.NORMAL) to HttpClient builder. Also changed MERIDIAN_AUTH_JWKS_URL in docker-compose to point directly to /oauth2/jwks.

bugs.error_message: Cerbos denies rm_jane all wealth agents: clearance-pii threshold was 3, rm_jane has clearance 2
bugs.root_cause: Cerbos policy required clearance >= 3 for confidential-pii. All wealth agents are data_classification=confidential-pii. rm_jane (and all standard RMs) have clearance 2.
bugs.fix: Changed confidential-pii threshold from >= 3 to >= 2. Portfolio PII is a core RM function; restricted trading data (clearance 3) is the right cutoff for 'restricted'.

bugs.error_message: E2E users rm_diaz and da_wpb do not exist in iam-service database
bugs.root_cause: Tests reference rm_diaz (dual-segment RM) and da_wpb (domain_admin) but only rm_jane was seeded in V2__seed_demo_data.sql
bugs.fix: Created new Flyway migration V3__seed_e2e_users.sql seeding both users with correct roles and attributes (clearance, segments, admin_domains)

bugs.error_message: 04-entitlements.spec.ts:12 and :51 timeout — 'Target page, context or browser has been closed' at helpers.ts:109
bugs.root_cause: Playwright test timeout was 90_000ms. LibreChat browser UI tests require ~15-20s login + ~60-80s LLM synthesis = 75-100s, exceeding the timeout. When timeout fires, Playwright closes the page context and page.waitForTimeout() throws 'page closed'.
bugs.fix: Increased timeout from 90_000 to 180_000ms in playwright.config.ts. Both UI tests now complete in ~90-96s and pass within 180s.

bugs.error_message: DomainManifestStore init fails: named capturing group is missing trailing } — Failed to parse domain manifest wealth-management.json

bugs.fix: Changed fallback to use Matcher.quoteReplacement(m.group(0)) so ${ is treated as literal text.

bugs.error_message: Insurance query naming POL-77001 wrongly clarifies 'which policy?' once a second domain is onboarded; gateway checks relationship_id instead of policy_id
bugs.root_cause: handleFetchData derived coverageEntity from coverageEntityType() which uses manifestStore.requiredContextKeys()/entityTypes() — the cross-domain UNION across ALL sub-domains — and picked the first key (relationship_id from wealth). The coverage entity was never scoped to the routed sub-domain.
bugs.fix: Added DomainManifestStore.entityTypesFor(subDomainId) (single sub-domain's entity_types) and scoped overloads coverageEntityType(EffectiveManifest)/secondaryEntityType(EffectiveManifest). In handleFetchData, once the resource-scoped agent's EffectiveManifest em is known, re-scope coverageEntity/secondaryEntity/carriedCoverageId from em.requiredContext()+em.subDomainId() before the coverage resolve/check/bind. Follow-up/session paths now use carriedCoverageEntityType(session) which finds whichever required entity the session actually carries (domain-agnostic).

bugs.error_message: LibreChat OIDC SSO: 'Callback authentication failed — server responded with an error in the response body'; login failed for rm_jane
bugs.root_cause: TWO bugs. (1) Client meridian-librechat registered only for CLIENT_SECRET_BASIC, but LibreChat's openid-client sends credentials via client_secret_post -> Spring AS rejected with 'Client authentication failed: authentication_method' -> invalid_client. (2) id_token/userinfo carried only `sub` because the JWT customizer enriched ACCESS_TOKEN only; LibreChat needs email/name to provision the user.
bugs.fix: (1) Added ClientAuthenticationMethod.CLIENT_SECRET_POST to the librechat RegisteredClient (alongside BASIC). (2) Extended JwtClaimsCustomizer to handle ID_TOKEN and added OidcClaimEnricher.enrichIdToken() returning email, email_verified, preferred_username, name (from principal + display_name attr). Verified full flow via curl: client_secret_post exchange -> HTTP 200, id_token+userinfo carry email/name, LibreChat callback -> 'login success', openid user created in Mongo.

Rewrote 4→17 lines (3 removed) | Also: user(["Relationship Manager"]); lc["LibreChat — chat UI
OpenAI-compatible clie | Also: > Create a detailed technical architecture diagram; > Manager" → a box "LibreChat (chat UI, OpenAI-com

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: List should be ArrayList

Gateway /v1/chat/completions accepts unauthenticated requests: no-identity resolves to 'anonymous' (HTTP 200), and an unknown/forged X-User-Id is processed. Auth is permitAll; only entitlement gates data.
bugs.root_cause: X-User-Id trusted-hop fallback exists because LibreChat can't forward the SSO JWT. permitAll + 'anonymous' default means requests without credentials still run (burn LLM calls); X-User-Id is forgeable. Safety currently rests on network isolation only.
bugs.fix: DEFERRED to the planned LibreChat rewrite (new UI will forward the JWT). Then: require valid JWT / reject anonymous+unknown (401); drop Redis PrincipalStore + seed. The proper JWT path already works (Axiom token -> Bearer -> gateway resolves principal from claims, no Redis). Interim safeguard: keep gateway network-isolated (not publicly exposed).
Changed to "Access denied for this client relationship."

bugs.error_message: QA §2 browser run: LibreChat hero answer rendered but #send-button stayed disabled beyond 180s, blocking follow-up. Pre-rebuild run also showed settlement/cash unavailable due MCP timeouts.
bugs.root_cause: Not fixed in this session. Evidence: Playwright screenshot shows rendered answer with disabled send button; gateway logs show full fan-out succeeded post-rebuild and AnswerSynthesizer call started, but LibreChat did not consider the stream complete within the QA timeout. Pre-rebuild servicing MCP emitted Starlette AssertionError and gateway timed out both MCP tools.
bugs.fix: Pending investigation. Check SSE completion/DONE delivery to LibreChat and whether LibreChat is waiting on a generation/title/summarize job despite titleConvo=false. Separately inspect servicing MCP ASGI middleware errors seen before rebuild.

bugs.error_message: Inline Python SSE parser commands failed with SyntaxError: unexpected character after line continuation character.
bugs.root_cause: Used single-quoted python -c strings containing literal \n sequences instead of shell $'...' quoting, so Python received backslash-n text.
bugs.fix: Reran the checks with shell $'...' quoting so Python received real newlines.

bugs.root_cause: No early return/throw for edge case: messages == null || messages.isEmpty(
bugs.fix: Added guard clause: if (messages == null || messages.isEmpty()

bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught

bugs.error_message: Multi-turn client pivot: naming a new client (Okafor) after another (Whitman) answered against the prior client — no re-resolution, no entitlement re-check (Codex QA #2/#3)
bugs.root_cause: (1) combined intent+extraction echoed the prior turn's resolved id from history as the entity reference; (2) coverage pipeline precedence inverted — session-carried id won over a client named this turn (resolve was only in the else-branch).
bugs.fix: (1) scope extraction to the LATEST user message + deterministic guard dropping extracted values matching the manifest id_pattern absent from that message; (2) flip precedence — a client named THIS turn resolves + re-authorizes; carried id reused only when none named.

bugs.error_message: mvn -q -Dtest=JwtClaimsCustomizerTest test failed: Mockito could not initialize inline Byte Buddy mock maker / could not self-attach to current VM
bugs.root_cause: The local JDK 23 runtime does not allow Mockito inline mock maker self-attachment in this sandboxed test run.
bugs.fix: Replaced Mockito usage with a tiny CapturingEnricher test fake that overrides OidcClaimEnricher methods directly.

bugs.error_message: mvn -q test failed: reference to assertThat is ambiguous for claims.getClaim("clearance")
bugs.root_cause: JwtClaimsSet#getClaim is generic, and AssertJ could not infer whether the raw numeric claim should use Predicate/IntPredicate overloads.
bugs.fix: Cast the claim to Integer before asserting equality.

bugs.error_message: python3 -m pytest tests/schema failed during pytest_configure with PermissionError: [Errno 1] Operation not permitted while pytest_rerunfailures tried to bind 127.0.0.1
bugs.root_cause: The sandbox blocks localhost socket binding during pytest plugin setup; the test suite itself had not collected yet.
bugs.fix: Reran the same targeted pytest command with approved unsandboxed execution; tests then collected and ran normally.

bugs.error_message: test_manifest_cross_references_are_consistent failed: acme.servicing.nav had sub_domain cash-management but was listed under corporate-actions
bugs.root_cause: The agent manifest sub_domain field drifted from the sub-domain manifest membership and existing docs.
bugs.fix: Changed acme.servicing.nav sub_domain to corporate-actions so agent, sub-domain manifest, and docs agree.

bugs.error_message: openwolf designqc --routes /login / failed because the auto-started dev server command `npm run dev` exited with code 127
bugs.root_cause: DesignQC's spawned dev-server environment could not execute npm even though npm and node are available on the interactive shell PATH.
bugs.fix: Recorded the tooling failure; UI changes continued with npm build verification planned.

bugs.error_message: npm run build failed with `sh: tsc: command not found`
bugs.root_cause: The admin-ui workspace had no installed node_modules, so the build script could not find the local TypeScript compiler.
bugs.fix: Ran npm ci from admin-ui using the existing package-lock, then reran npm run build successfully.

bugs.error_message: Vite dev server failed with `listen EPERM: operation not permitted 127.0.0.1:5174`; sandboxed OpenWolf DesignQC browser launch also failed with `Failed to launch the browser process`
bugs.root_cause: The managed sandbox blocked local port listening and browser process launch for screenshot QA.
bugs.fix: Reran npm run dev with approved escalation, then reran OpenWolf DesignQC with approved browser escalation and captured screenshots successfully.

bugs.error_message: npm run build failed in admin-ui: sh: tsc: command not found
bugs.root_cause: admin-ui node_modules were absent in this worktree, so the local TypeScript binary from devDependencies was not installed.
bugs.fix: Ran npm ci in admin-ui, then reran npm run build successfully.

bugs.error_message: Workbench slice review: not mergeable as a giant page/root API file; native EventSource cannot send Authorization headers; non-streaming chat should be labeled MVP.
bugs.root_cause: Initial slice concentrated page state, API types, formatting, trace parsing, and panel components in admin-ui/src/pages/Workbench.tsx and used native EventSource.
bugs.fix: Refactored into a feature module with typed api.ts/types.ts, hooks, utilities, focused components, a tiny page wrapper, route-level lazy loading, fetch-based SSE with Authorization headers, and an explicit MVP non-streaming chat label.

bugs.error_message: npm run build failed in admin-ui: src/features/workbench/api.ts(12,34): error TS2339: Property 'env' does not exist on type 'ImportMeta'.
bugs.root_cause: The app did not have Vite client env typings, so TypeScript did not know import.meta.env.
bugs.fix: Added admin-ui/src/vite-env.d.ts with vite/client reference and a typed non-secret VITE_GATEWAY_API_BASE override.

bugs.error_message: Admin UI login stored JWT under meridian_admin_token, but the shared API client read conduit_admin_token, causing authenticated admin API calls to omit Authorization.
bugs.root_cause: Token storage key drift between the auth provider and API client.
bugs.fix: Added admin-ui/src/auth/tokenStorage.ts and wired useAuth, the IAM API client, and Workbench gateway API to the same read/write/clear helpers with legacy key fallback.

bugs.error_message: Gateway used cross-turn ConversationSession memory despite exposing a stateless OpenAI-compatible API, manufacturing pivot/precedence/reset bugs.
bugs.root_cause: Resolved entities and last agent results were stored by conversationId in Redis, then reused on later turns. A stale carried entity could override a newly named entity or survive a client reset.
bugs.fix: Made gateway a pure per-request function of messages[] + JWT: removed session store reads/writes, deleted ConversationSession/ConversationSessionStore, removed fixed history truncation, enforced latest user mention wins over user-authored messages, re-resolve/re-check coverage every turn, and clarify deterministically when no user-authored resource reference exists.

bugs.error_message: Workbench sent the logged-in admin JWT to the gateway, so entitlement decisions reflected admin instead of the selected demo persona.
bugs.root_cause: Workbench had no Axiom-minted persona token exchange and reused readAdminToken() for chat and trace requests.
bugs.fix: Added platform-admin-gated /auth/impersonate with audited short-lived JWTs, wired Workbench persona selection from usersApi.list(), and send persona Bearer tokens to chat plus trace stream.

bugs.error_message: Users page rendered blank because the UI consumed /users as User[] while IAM returns PageResponse<UserResponse>.
bugs.root_cause: usersApi.list() returned the raw paginated response, so Users.tsx called array methods on an object and crashed the route without a boundary.
bugs.fix: Mapped usersApi.list() to PageResponse.content, added route ErrorBoundary, visible users error state, and corrected create/update payloads to match IAM DTOs.

bugs.error_message: Trace rail could show events from other conversations and retained old events after New conversation.
bugs.root_cause: Client whitelisted null conversationId events and the gateway trace stream broadcast every event to every subscriber; local event state was append-only across conversation resets.
bugs.fix: Added optional server-side conversationId filtering for trace subscribers, made Workbench connect with the current conversationId, dropped non-matching events, and cleared events/request tracking when persona or conversation changes.

bugs.error_message: PolicyForge submitted role UUIDs as subject_roles instead of Cerbos role names.
bugs.root_cause: The UI treated RoleResponse.id as the policy role identifier, but Cerbos policies and the policy generator expect role names.
bugs.fix: Changed policy role selection to store role.name, surfaced policy data load errors, and kept the Axiom/gold visual treatment consistent.

bugs.error_message: Sandboxed smoke/Maven/Python test attempts produced localhost socket errors unrelated to product behavior.
bugs.root_cause: macOS Codex sandbox denied localhost sockets for curl/Python/Redis-backed tests.
bugs.fix: Stopped/reran the invalid smoke attempt and reran smoke, Maven gateway tests, Playwright, and pytest integration with approved unsandboxed execution.

bugs.error_message: Glass-box trace for Okafor denial did not show an explicit entitlement CHECK deny event; trace jumped from agents_resolved to request_complete agentCount=0.
bugs.root_cause: Coverage/entitlement denial is not emitted as a first-class trace event in the captured stream.
bugs.fix: Not fixed in this run; emit CHECK/coverage-deny trace data before completing denied requests.

bugs.fix: Not fixed in this run; include persona coverage options in clarify responses or update TEST-KIT expectation.

bugs.error_message: Canonical :8099 Playwright login specs fail waiting for legacy #email selector after redirect to Axiom Username/Password login
bugs.root_cause: The checked-in browser specs still target the legacy LibreChat email/password login contract and default URLs, while the canonical chat app redirects to Axiom OIDC at host.docker.internal:8084/login.
bugs.fix: No code changed in this review pass; update e2e helpers/specs to use :8099, Axiom Username/Password selectors, and the new OIDC flow.

bugs.error_message: Admin console browser login on :5182 returns 403 Invalid CORS request
bugs.root_cause: Browser-origin POST from http://localhost:5182/login to /api/auth/login includes Origin/Referer and is rejected as CORS, while the same proxied login without Origin succeeds with 200.
bugs.fix: No code changed in this review pass; align IAM/admin-console CORS/proxy config so http://localhost:5182 is an allowed origin for browser login.

bugs.error_message: conduit-chat logged post-stream persistence failed when browser was closed mid-stream during summary test
bugs.root_cause: The test closed the browser while the final streamed response was still active; the BFF logged InterruptedException and failed post-stream persistence for that conversation.
bugs.fix: No code changed in this review pass; consider making stream-abort persistence/idempotency robust, or classify this as expected client-abort behavior if acceptable.
